text:
Deep in the belly of a vast forest, under the weight of centuries, there lived a tiny fairy named Felicity. She was no giant, but she was undaunted, her petite figure adorned with the finest shimmering wings that danced with every breath of the forest’s mists.

One dew-adorned dawn, as Felicity flew through the dappled sunlight filtering through the leaves, she stumbled upon a clearing unlike any she'd previously seen. In the center was a peculiar tree, ancient beyond measure, its bark cracked and twisted, yet it seemed to lean ever so slightly towards the center of the clearing. It was from this tree that Felicity noticed a phenomenon — a pool of luminescent water that glowed softly, flickering like constellations in a starry sky.

With curiosity piqued and a gentle heart, Felicity approached the tree. It whispered secrets only the wind could carry—a tale of a time when the balance of the forest was far more delicate
